evals.name: props-emits-design
evals.prompt: I'm building a reusable StatusBadge component. It takes a status and should tell the parent when it's clicked. How should I define its props and events?

assertions.text: Recommends object-syntax defineProps for status with type and a validator constraining allowed values, and a factory default for any object/array prop
Declares the click event via defineEmits and emits it, noting props are read-only one-way data flow and undeclared events fall through as attributes

evals.name: component-v-model
evals.prompt: I have a custom SearchField component wrapping a v-text-field. How do I make it work with v-model from the parent?

assertions.text: Uses defineModel() returning a ref bound to the inner v-text-field with v-model, instead of hand-writing modelValue prop + update:modelValue event
Notes the parent uses <SearchField v-model="query" /> and advises against a child-side default that can desync the parent

evals.name: scoped-slot-list
evals.prompt: I want a DataList component where the parent controls how each row is rendered but the child owns the data fetching. What slot pattern do I use?

assertions.text: Recommends a scoped slot exposing each item via slot props (e.g. <slot :item="item" />) so parent template content can read child-owned data
Shows the parent consuming it with #default="{ item }" (or v-slot) and mentions a stable :key on the slotted v-for

evals.name: negative-reactivity-routing
evals.prompt: Should I use ref() or reactive() for a form state object, and when does a computed re-evaluate?

assertions.text: Defers to the vue3-composition skill: ref vs reactive and computed re-evaluation are reactivity primitives, not component communication design owned by vue3-components
